sections.paragraph:
ἰσχυρότης, ητος, ἡ (s. prec. and next entry; Dionys. Hal. 3, 65, 2 Jac. v.l.; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 204) power, (inward) strength προσῆλθεν ὑμῖν ἰ. you received power Hv 3, 12, 3. Of stones: solidity, strength (cp. Job 6:12 ἰσχὺς λίθων) Hs 9, 8, 7.—DELG s.v. ἰσχύς.

prec. prec. = preceding

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 484
